info.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM - Journeys/Tasks
info.description:
Last Updated January 2026

You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

Whether you're experienced with or new to REST, use this guide to find what you need, including:

  • A quick start how-to that walks you through a simple request example.
  • Use cases that provide real-world solutions with code examples.
  • Detailed descriptions for each REST resource.
info.x-summary: Last Updated January 2026 You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

get.summary: Get all tasks in a journey template

parameters.name: JourneyId
parameters.description: The value of this parameter could be a hash of the key that is used to uniquely identify the resource item. The client should not generate the hash key value.  Instead, the client should query on the collection resource with a filter to navigate to a specific resource item. For example: products?q=InventoryItemId=<value1>

schema.description: Primary key of the journey template.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: When this parameter is provided, the specified children are included in the resource payload (instead of just a link). The value of this query parameter is "all" or "". More than one child can be specified using comma as a separator. Example: ?expand=Employees,Localizations. Nested children can also be provided following the format "Child.NestedChild" (Example: ?expand=Employees.Managers). If a nested child is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?expand=Employees.Managers" is the same as "?expand=Employees,Employees.Managers" (which will expand Employees and Managers).

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
This parameter filters the resource fields. Only the specified fields are returned, which means that if no fields are specified, no fields are returned (useful to get only the links). If an indirect child resource is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?fields=Employees.Managers:Empname" is the same as "?fields=;Employees:;Employees.Managers:Empname" (which will only return the "Empname" field for Managers). the value of this query parameter is a list of resource fields. The attribute can be a direct (Example: Employees) or indirect (Example: Employees.Managers) child. It cannot be combined with expand query parameter. If both are provided, only fields will be considered.

Format: ?fields=Attribute1,Attribute2

Format for fields in child resource: ?fields=Accessor1:Attribute1,Attribute2

parameters.name: onlyData
parameters.description: The resource item payload will be filtered in order to contain only data (no links section, for example).

parameters.name: dependency
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies dependencies which are fields that are set before and rolled back after generating the response. Generally they are used to preview the effects of an attribute change. The fields specified in this parameter are always set in the resource instance in question. When a child resource collection is requested and the parameter is set, the fields will be set in the parent resource instance before generating the resource collection payload. The value of this query parameter is a set of dependency fields. Example: dependency=ProductId=2

Format: <attr1>=<val1>,<attr2>=<value2>

parameters.name: links
parameters.description:
This parameter can be used to show only certain links while accessing a singular resource or a resource collection. The parameter value format is a comma-separated list of : <link_relation>

Example:
self,canonical

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: This parameter restricts the number of resources returned inside the resource collection. If the limit exceeds the resource count then the framework will only return the available resources.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Used to define the starting position of the resource collection. If offset exceeds the resource count then no resources are returned. Default value is 0.

parameters.name: totalResults
parameters.description: The resource collection representation will include the "estimated row count" when "?totalResults=true", otherwise the count is not included.  The default value is "false".

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
This query parameter defines the where clause. The resource collection will be queried using the provided expressions. The value of this query parameter is one or more expressions. Example: ?q=Deptno>=10 and <= 30;Loc!=NY

Format: ?q=expression1;expression2

You can use these queryable attributes to filter this collection resource using the q query parameter:
  • JourneyTaskId; integer; Unique identifier of the journey task.
  • LearningContentTypeMeaning; string; Meaning of the learning content type.
  • Name; string; Name of the journey task.
  • ReminderContentType; string; Content type of the task reminder notification.
  • RequiredFlag; boolean; Indicates whether the task is mandatory or optional. Valid values are Yes and No. Default is No.
  • Status; string; Code for the task status.
  • TaskCode; string; Code of a task in a journey template.
  • VideoTypeMeaning; string; Meaning of video type.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: This parameter orders a resource collection based on the specified fields.  The parameter value is a comma-separated string of attribute names, each optionally followed by a colon and "asc" or "desc".  Specify "asc" for ascending and "desc" for descending. The default value is "asc".  For example, ?orderBy=field1:asc,field2:desc

parameters.name: finder
parameters.description:
Used as a predefined finder to search the collection.

Format ?finder=<finderName>;<variableName>=<variableValue>,<variableName2>=<variableValue2>

The following are the available finder names and corresponding finder variables

  • PrimaryKey Finds all journey tasks based on primary key.
    Finder Variables
    • JourneyTaskId; integer; Unique identifier of the journey task.
  • findActiveTasks Finds all active journey tasks.
    Finder Variables

x-adf-finder-formats.name: PrimaryKey
x-adf-finder-formats.description: Finds all journey tasks based on primary key.

JourneyTaskId.description: Unique identifier of the journey task.

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

post.summary: Create a task in a journey template

parameters.name: JourneyId
parameters.description: The value of this parameter could be a hash of the key that is used to uniquely identify the resource item. The client should not generate the hash key value.  Instead, the client should query on the collection resource with a filter to navigate to a specific resource item. For example: products?q=InventoryItemId=<value1>

schema.description: Primary key of the journey template.

parameters.name: Upsert-Mode
parameters.description: Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, the server performs an Upsert operation instead of a Create operation. During an Upsert operation, the server attempts to find an existing resource that matches the payload. If a match is found, the server updates the existing resource instead of creating a new one. If not found or false (default), the server performs a Create operation. Note that the Upsert operation isn't supported for date-effective REST resources.

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

notifications.description: The notifications resource is a child of the tasks resource. It includes notification settings for the task performer and owner.

properties.description: The properties resource is the child of the journeys resource. It includes all the task type properties associated with the parent journey template.

count.description: The number of resource instances returned in the current range.

hasMore.description: Indicates whether more resources are available on the server than the subset returned in the response. If the value is true, then there are more resources to retrieve from the server. The default value is false.

limit.description: The actual paging size used by the server.

links.title: Links
links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

offset.description: The offset value used in the current page.

totalResults.description: The estimated row count when "?totalResults=true", otherwise the count is not included.

ActionAddToCalendarLabel.description: Setting that enables to change the label of the add to calendar action.

ActionCompleteLabel.description: Setting that enables to change the label of the complete action.

ActionRejectLabel.description: Setting that enables to change the label of the not applicable action.

ActionSaveLabel.description: Setting that enables to change the label of the save as draft action.

ActionURL.title: URL
ActionURL.description: The URL associated with the task that's entered on the setup screen. To retrieve a navigable URL, use the getNavigationURL custom method of the resource.

ActivationEligibilityProfileId.description: Identifier of the eligibility profile used for the activation criteria in a journey task.

ActivationEligibilityProfileName.title: Name
ActivationEligibilityProfileName.description: Name of the eligibility profile used for the activation criteria in a journey task.

ActivityAction1Label.description: Setting that enables to change the label of action 1.

ActivityAction2Label.description: Setting that enables to change the label of action 2.

ActivityAction3Label.description: Setting that enables to change the label of action 3.

ActivityAction4Label.description: Setting that enables to change the label of action 4.

ActivityAction5Label.description: Setting that enables to change the label of action 5.

AddToLibraryFlag.description: Indicates whether the task can be added to personal task library. Valid values are Yes or No. The default is No.

AllowAttachmentFlag.description: Indicates whether attachments can be added to the task. Valid values are Yes or No. The default is No.

AllowCommentFlag.description: Indicates whether comments can be added to the task. Valid values are Yes or No. The default is No.

AllowNoteTitleFlag.description: Indicates whether the Notes title can be added. Valid values are Yes or No. The default is No.

AllowNotesContentFlag.description: Indicates whether rich content can be added for notes. Valid values are Yes or No. The default is Yes.

AnalysisParameters.description: Parameters associated with an analytics task type.

AnalysisPath.description: OTBI analysis path for an analytics task type.

AttachmentDocumentTypeId.description: Description for the AttachmentDocumentTypeId attribute in the tasks resource, which is part of the journeys root resource.

AttachmentDocumentTypeName.title: Document Type
AttachmentDocumentTypeName.description: Description for the AttachmentDocumentTypeName attribute in the tasks resource, which is part of the journeys root resource.

CompletedFeedFlag.description: Indicates whether the atom feed is enabled when a journey task is completed.

ConfigurableFormContext.title: Configurable Form
ConfigurableFormContext.description: Context for configurable form task type.

ContactUserId.description: User ID of the task performer.

ContactUserIdentifier.description: Internal identifier of the journey task performer.

ContentProviderCode.description: Code of the user-defined content task.

DelayDurationUOM.description: Unit of measure used in specifying delaying the duration.

DelayDurationUOMMeaning.title: Meaning
DelayDurationUOMMeaning.description: Meaning of unit of measure used in delay duration.

DeletedFeedFlag.description: Indicates whether the atom feed is enabled when a journey task is deleted.

DependentTaskId.description: Primary key or unique identifier of the dependent task.

DigitalSignatureTemplateId.description: Identifier of the digital signature template.

DocumentTypeId.title: Document Type
DocumentTypeId.description: Description for the document type identifier attribute. Identifier of the document type.

EmbeddedApplicationTaskCode.description: Code of the embedded application task.

EmbeddedApplicationTaskTypeId.description: Unique identifier of the embedded application task.

EnableExpiryFlag.description: Indicates whether the task should expire. Valid values are Yes and No. The default value is No.

EvaluationOffset.description: Number of days to consider for eligibility evaluation.

ExpiryDuration.title: Expiry Days
ExpiryDuration.description: Duration after which the task expires.

ExpiryRelativeTo.description: Criteria based on which the task expires.

ExpiryRelativeToMeaning.title: Meaning
ExpiryRelativeToMeaning.description: Meaning of the criteria based on which the task expires.

FeedFlag.description: Indicates whether the atom feed is enabled for a journey task.

GuidedJourneyTaskCodes.description: Code of the guided journey task.

InitiatedFeedFlag.description: Indicates whether the atom feed is enabled when a journey task is initiated.

JourneyTaskId.description: Unique identifier of the journey task.

LastUpdateDate.description: Date on which task was last updated.

LastUpdatedBy.description: User who last updated the task.

LearningContentTypeMeaning.description: Meaning of the learning content type.

LearningItemId.description: Unique identifier of the learning item.

Notes.description: Notes for the task. The notes text is stored as a CLOB object. To use in the payload, the text should be converted to a base64 encoded string using either a conversion tool or a prerequisite script.

OpenInSamePageFlag.description: Indicates whether the external URL should open in the existing page or a new page. Valid values are Yes and No. The default value is No.

OpenReportInSamePageFlag.description: Indicates whether the report should open in the existing page or a new page. Valid values are Yes and No. The default value is Yes.

OwnerPersonId.description: Internal identifier of the journey task owner.

OwnerResponsibilityType.description: Responsibility type of the journey task owner.

OwnerResponsibilityTypeMeaning.description: Responsibility type meaning of the journey task owner.

OwnerUserGroupCode.description: Code of the owner user group.

PerformerPersonId.description: Internal identifier of the journey task performer.

PerformerResponsibilityType.description: Responsibility type of the journey task performer.

PerformerResponsibilityTypeMeaning.description: Responsibility type meaning of the journey task performer.

PerformerUserGroupCode.description: Code of the performer user group.

ReassignedFeedFlag.description: Indicates whether the atom feed is enabled when a journey task is reassigned.

ReminderContentDescription.description: Content description of the task reminder notification.

ReminderContentTitle.description: Content title of the task reminder notification.

ReminderContentType.description: Content type of the task reminder notification.

ReminderRelativeToMeaning.title: Meaning
ReminderRelativeToMeaning.description: Meaning of reminder relative to task duration.

ReminderTemplateId.description: Primary key or unique identifier of the reminder notification template.

ReportPath.title: Report Path
ReportPath.description: Path to the report in BI Catalog.

RequiredFlag.title: Required
RequiredFlag.description: Indicates whether the task is mandatory or optional. Valid values are Yes and No. Default is No.

SaveAttachmentsToDORFor.description: Document records of person for whom to save task attachments.

SaveAttachmentsToDORForMeaning.description: Meaning of save attachments to document records.

SaveDocumentsToDORFor.description: Attachments to add to document records for a document type.

SaveDocumentsToDORForMeaning.description: Meaning of add attachments document records.

Sequence.description: Task display ordering sequence. Default is 1.

SourceTaskId.description: Primary key or unique identifier of the source task.

SubType.title: Signature Type
SubType.description: Task action subtype code. Available only for action types I-9 and Electronic Signature.

SubTypeMeaning.description: Task action subtype. Available only for action types I9 and Electronic Signature.

TargetDurationUOM.title: UOM
TargetDurationUOM.description: Unit of measure for the target duration.

TargetDurationUOMMeaning.title: Meaning
TargetDurationUOMMeaning.description: Meaning of the unit of measure used in the target duration.

TaskCode.description: Code of a task in a journey template.

TaskGroupId.description: Unique identifier of the journey task group.

Metadata-Context.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

REST-Framework-Version.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.
